title: CBIC Clarifies GST Jurisdiction for Migrating Taxpayers
content:
# CBIC Clarifies GST Jurisdiction for Migrating Taxpayers

Ek important update hai. Government ne GST jurisdiction ke clarification ke liye naya circular announce kiya hai, especially jab taxable persons apna Principal Place of Business change karte hain.

Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) ne Circular No. 255/01/2026-GST dated June 25, 2026, issue kiya hai, jismein GST jurisdiction se related ambiguities ko address kiya gaya hai in cases where a registered taxpayer migrates or transfers their principal place of business from one jurisdiction to another.

**Key Clarifications:**

*   **Validity of Prior Actions:** Any action or proceeding validly initiated by the *transferor* (original) jurisdictional authority, having jurisdiction over the registered taxpayer before their migration, will remain legally valid.
*   **Role of New Jurisdiction:** The *transferee* (new) jurisdictional authority shall take over and conclude all pending proceedings from the stage at which they stood at the time of migration/transfer. They will also be competent to take all further actions, including consequential proceedings.
*   **Restriction on Old Authority:** Post-migration, the transferor jurisdictional authority shall not initiate any fresh proceedings or take further action against the taxpayer. Any new issue noticed by the transferor authority should be communicated to the transferee jurisdictional authority for appropriate action.
*   **Ensuring Continuity:** This clarification aims to ensure continuity of proceedings, prevent jurisdictional disputes, and promote uniform implementation of the CGST Act across field formations.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.caalley.com/circulars-2026/circular-no-255-01-2026-gst.html)

*Published on: 27 June 2026*
